_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX RGV state House batch HD-39..43: Armando Martinez (D), Terry Canales (D), Robert Guerra (D, RETIRING), Richard Raymond (D), J.M. Lozano (R). State tier (49 cells). TRUE=documented aligned, FALSE=documented contrary, null=no evidence. Marquee votes verified via TX House Journal PDFs or by-exclusion from named crossover lists; issue-group scorecards (Texas Right to Life, Texas Values Action, Texas Alliance for Life, ATPE Teach the Vote, Texans for Fiscal Responsibility) as HARD aggregate.

armando-martinez@TX.notes_set: Long-serving RGV Democrat (HD-39, Hidalgo Co., in office since 2005); running unopposed in 2026 (GOP primary canceled). Uniform progressive voting record on this rubric, verified against TX House Journal roll calls: NAY on SB8 Heartbeat Act (2021) and HB1280 trigger ban (2021); NAY on SB14 minors gender-transition ban (2023); NAY on SB4 illegal-entry state crime (2023); NAY on HB1927 constitutional carry (2021). Voted to strip ESA vouchers from HB1 (2023 special). Participated in the 2021 Democratic quorum break to block election bill SB1. Texas Right to Life 36% (2025); Texas Values Action 28% (2025). No documented conservative deviation. No reliable church affiliation found.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB8 (Heartbeat Act, RV784) and HB1280 (trigger ban, RV793), 2021 — contrary to life-from-conception protection.

1.note: Voted against the Human Life Protection Act / trigger ban; Texas Right to Life 36% (2025). No abolition advocacy.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB14 (2023 ban on gender-transition procedures for minors, RV1688); Texas Values Action 28% (2025).

0.note: Voted with all Democrats to strip the ESA/voucher provision from HB1 (2023 special session, 84-63) — opposed school choice.

1.note: Participated in the 2021 Democratic quorum break to block SB1, the omnibus election bill that strengthened mail-ballot ID — contrary to voter-ID position.

3.note: Quorum break opposed SB1, which restricted mail voting, drop boxes, and drive-thru voting — contrary to opposing mass mail-in/harvesting.

1.note: Voted NAY on SB4 (2023, illegal entry as a state crime authorizing removal, RV48) — contrary to deportation enforcement.

2.note: SB4 NAY — contrary to anti-sanctuary state enforcement / preemption of non-cooperation.

0.note: Voted NAY on HB1927 (2021 permitless/constitutional carry, RV298).

terry-canales@TX.notes_set: RGV Democrat (HD-40, Edinburg, since 2013), running for re-election 2026 (won unopposed primary). Mostly progressive on this rubric but with two documented cross-party deviations: (1) JOINT AUTHOR of HB1927 constitutional carry (2021) with a pro-bill floor speech; (2) YES votes on SB10 Ten Commandments in classrooms and SB11 prayer/Bible time in schools (2025). Otherwise: voted against pro-life bills SB33 and SB1233 (2025); voted for repeal of the homosexual-conduct law (HB1738), Equality Texas A+ (2021); NO on SB14 (2023 gender-ban, by exclusion from named defectors); NO on SB2 ESA vouchers (2025); opposed SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 and HB900 library-content bills; opposed SB1 (2021 election law, quorum break); opposed HB7 (2023 border security funding, Texans for Fiscal Responsibility F). Texas Values Action 23%, Texas Alliance for Life 37% (the 37% reflects the bipartisan 134-4 SB31 Life-of-the-Mother medical-exception bill, not a pro-life conversion). Publicly professes faith in Jesus Christ.

1.note: Opposed SB33 (no taxpayer funds for abortion travel) and SB1233 (perinatal palliative care), 2025 — contrary to life position; no abolition advocacy. (Texas Alliance for Life 37% reflects bipartisan SB31, not a pro-life record.)
Voted for HB1738 repealing the homosexual-conduct statute (against Texas Values position); Equality Texas A+ (2021).

2.note: NO on SB14 (2023 minors gender-transition ban, by exclusion from the four named Democratic defectors); opposed HB229 'What is a Woman Act' (2025).

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA voucher / school choice).

1.note: Opposed SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights) — contrary to parental notification/consent expansion.

2.note: Opposed SB13 (2025) and HB900 (2023) library-content/READER bills restricting sexual content — contrary to opposing sexualized content in schools.

3.note: Voted YES on SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms) and SB11 (prayer/Bible-reading time in schools), 2025; supported SCR19 Texas Life Monument. (Cross-party deviation; he did oppose SB965 employee-prayer bill.)

1.note: Opposed SB1 (2021 election law / voter ID); joined the Democratic quorum break to D.C. to block it and stated he would vote against it.

3.note: SB1 opposition — contrary to restricting mass mail-in voting and drop boxes.

0.note: Voted against HB7 (2023 border security funding, including barrier/Operation Lone Star appropriations) per Texans for Fiscal Responsibility index.
JOINT AUTHOR of HB1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) with a pro-bill floor speech framing it as criminal-justice reform. Cross-party deviation.

robert-guerra@TX.notes_set: HD-41 Democrat (Hidalgo Co., McAllen, since 2012). RETIRING: announced 2025-10-02 he will not seek re-election; term ends 2026-01-12 (seat now open, both-party 2026 runoffs). Uniform party-line progressive record: NO on SB14 (2023 gender-ban, by exclusion from named defectors); NO on HB1927 constitutional carry (2021, by exclusion from the seven named Democratic Yes votes); consistent anti-voucher record incl. NO on SB2 (2025 ESA); opposed SB12, SB13, HB900. Texas Right to Life 0% (2025); Texas Values Action 9%; Texas Alliance for Life 43%; Texas AFL-CIO 90%. No conservative deviation found. No documented church affiliation.

1.note: Texas Right to Life 0% (2025), 15% (2023) — consistently anti-life voting record; no abolition advocacy.

2.note: NO on SB14 (2023 minors gender-transition ban) — not among the four named Democratic defectors; Texas Values Action 9% (2025).

0.note: Consistent anti-voucher record: NO on SB2 (2025 ESA), supported 2023/2021 amendments banning voucher funding.

1.note: Opposed SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights).

2.note: Opposed SB13 (2025) and HB900 (2023) library-content bills.

0.note: NO on HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry) — not among the seven named Democratic Yes votes.

richard-raymond@TX.notes_set: Long-serving Laredo Democrat (HD-42 / formerly HD-44, since 1993; sitting in the 89th Leg, running 2026). Rated the most conservative House Democrat by Young Conservatives of Texas (44, 88th session), but the documented record is thin and his abortion record is pro-choice, NOT pro-life as sometimes assumed. HARD findings: voted FOR HB1927 constitutional carry (2021) — one of only three Democrats on the final conference report (cross-party deviation); Texas Right to Life 0%, voted against the 2011 sonogram bill and a 2011 abortion-restriction bill; voted against school-choice (kept the 2023 anti-voucher prohibition); voted against property-tax-relief bills (Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career F). Several marquee 2021-2025 roll calls (SB8, SB14, SB4, SB1) could not be individually verified and are left null rather than inferred. No documented church affiliation. NOTE: 2026 primary result not yet confirmed.

0.note: Voted against the 2011 mandatory pre-abortion sonogram bill and a 2011 abortion-restriction bill — contrary to life-from-conception protection.

1.note: Texas Right to Life 0%; voted against abortion restrictions. No abolition advocacy.

0.note: Voted against school choice — opposed the HB1 amendment that would have removed the prohibition on voucher funding (2023).
Voted FOR HB1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) — one of only three Democrats supporting the final conference report. Cross-party deviation.

jose-manuel-lozano@TX.notes_set: HD-43 Republican (Kingsville / South Texas; elected as a Democrat in 2010, switched to GOP in 2012). Running for re-election 2026. Socially conservative, evidence-cited: self-described 'unwavering pro-life'; COAUTHOR of HB3399 (2025 ban on gender-transition care); voted FOR SB2 ESA vouchers (2025, by exclusion — only Phelan and VanDeaver among Republicans voted no) and was not among the 2023 anti-voucher Republican defectors; voted FOR HB1927 constitutional carry (2021, by exclusion — only Meyer among Republicans voted no); self-stated support for $5B+ border security incl. barriers. Texas Values Action 94% 'Faith & Family Champion' (2025). The documented cross-party deviation is FISCAL: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade F (2011-2023), YCT 36 — among the most fiscally moderate House Republicans; he also voted to impeach AG Ken Paxton in 2023 (later publicly regretted, coauthored an apology resolution). Listed as Baptist.

0.note: Self-stated: 'I'm unwavering in my pro-life stance.'

2.note: COAUTHOR of HB3399 (2025, ban on gender-transition procedures); Texas Values Action 94% 'Faith & Family Champion'.

0.note: Voted FOR SB2 ESA vouchers (2025) — only Phelan and VanDeaver among Republicans voted no; not among the 2023 anti-voucher Republican defectors.

2.note: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade F (2011-2023); YCT 36 — among the most fiscally moderate House Republicans, voting for spending against fiscal-restraint positions. Cross-party (fiscal) deviation.

0.note: Self-stated: 'proud to support over $5 billion in funding for border security, including more law enforcement personnel and land and water barriers along the Texas-Mexico border.'
Voted FOR HB1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) — only Morgan Meyer among House Republicans voted no.
